calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Vacated

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT KYLE TSE, AND NANXI LIU's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Motion For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Kyle Tse and Nanxi Liu seek an order compelling plaintiff David Mar to provide further responses to their Special Interrogatories, Set One, and for sanctions. Mar has not filed an opposition. The court grants the motion as set forth herein.

Mar's objections to the following special interrogatories lack merit and he is ordered to provide further responses: Nos. 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13. The court does not find any invasion of constitutional privacy interests with these interrogatories, the chief objection interposed by Mar. With respect to No. 6, the mere identification of documents exchanged with potential renters does not implicate privacy concerns, although the production of those documents might. Mar is ordered to amend his responses to these interrogatories within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Tse and Liu also seek sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are permitted even where a party does not oppose a discovery motion that is granted against it. (Rules of Court, 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders Mar to pay $990 in sanctions to Tse and Liu within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
